title: European VC funding narrows around a handful of AI bets
excerpt: Ixefra/Getty Images European VC is concentrating; funding this year is pacing ahead of 2025, but across fewer vehicles, and more than half of that money is g...
content_html:
Leah Hodgson

Fri, July 17, 2026 at 6:58 AM CDT 2 min read

Ixefra/Getty Images

European VC is concentrating; funding this year is pacing ahead of 2025, but across fewer vehicles, and more than half of that money is going to AI startups.

According to PitchBook's Q2 2026 European Venture Report, deal value reached €44 billion (about $50 billion) in H1, putting the continent's startups on track to raise 27% more than last year. Of that, €26.5 billion is in the AI sector, where funding has already surpassed last year's annual total. While AI accounted for 37.9% of deal value in 2025, it now represents 60.3%.

The total number of rounds has dropped. Mega-rounds—worth more than €100 million—accounted for more than half of deal value in Q2, compared to around 37% in 2025.

Seven of the largest-ever European VC deals on record this year. London-based AI infrastructure startup Nscale raised €2.9 billion in funding this year, making it the region's largest deal. AI drug designer Isomorphic Labs' $2.1 billion Series B and German robotics developer Neura Robotics' $1.4 billion Series C also make the list.

It's not just the later stages that are seeing hefty rounds. This year, two startups have closed seed rounds of $1 billion or more. French world model developer AMI Labs secured $1.03 billion in Europe's largest-ever seed investment in March, only to be surpassed by London-based AI lab Ineffable Intelligence's $1.1 billion round a month later.

The concentration in capital is extending to investors. Capital raised in H1 reached €8.2 billion, pacing 32.8% above last year, while fund count continues to fall. The median size of a European VC vehicle has jumped to €60 million this year, compared to €50 million in 2025.

Paris-based biopharma-focused Jeito Capital's $1.2 billion second fund was the largest to close this year and the only fund to have raised more than €1 billion. London-based Hummingbird Ventures' $600 million growth fund followed.

While capital is flowing into fewer funds, it is not just being reserved for experienced managers. Emerging VCs accounted for 52.8% of capital raised, and they are pacing ahead of last year's total by a larger percentage than experienced firms.

This article originally appeared on PitchBook News

content_html_zh:
利亚·霍奇森

2026 年 7 月 17 日星期五上午 6:58 CDT 阅读 2 分钟

伊克斯夫拉/盖蒂图片社

欧洲风险投资正在集中；今年的融资进度将提前于 2025 年进行，但涉及的车辆数量较少，而且其中一半以上的资金将流向人工智能初创公司。

根据 PitchBook 的 2026 年第二季度欧洲风险投资报告，上半年交易价值达到 440 亿欧元（约合 500 亿美元），这使得欧洲大陆的初创企业有望比去年增长 27%。 其中，265 亿欧元用于人工智能领域，该领域的资金已经超过了去年的年度总额。 2025 年，人工智能占交易价值的 37.9%，目前已占 60.3%。

总回合数有所下降。 价值超过 1 亿欧元的大型融资占第二季度交易价值的一半以上，而 2025 年这一比例约为 37%。

今年有七笔有史以来最大的欧洲风险投资交易。 总部位于伦敦的人工智能基础设施初创公司 Nscale 今年筹集了 29 亿欧元的资金，成为该地区最大的一笔交易。 人工智能药物设计公司 Isomorphic Labs 的 21 亿美元 B 轮融资和德国机器人开发商 Neura Robotics 的 14 亿美元 C 轮融资也榜上有名。

不仅仅是后期阶段出现大量回合。 今年，两家初创公司已经完成了 10 亿美元或更多的种子轮融资。 法国世界模型开发商 AMI Labs 在 3 月份获得了欧洲有史以来最大的种子投资 10.3 亿美元，但一个月后，伦敦人工智能实验室 Ineffable Intelligence 的 11 亿美元融资超过了这一投资。

资本的集中正在向投资者延伸。 上半年筹集的资金达到 82 亿欧元，比去年增长 32.8%，但资金数量继续下降。 欧洲风险投资公司的中位规模今年已跃升至 6000 万欧元，而 2025 年为 5000 万欧元。

总部位于巴黎、专注于生物制药的 Jeito Capital 旗下第二支基金规模达 12 亿美元，是今年募集规模最大的基金，也是唯一一支筹集超过 10 亿欧元的基金。 总部位于伦敦的 Hummingbird Ventures 推出了 6 亿美元的成长基金。

虽然资本流入的基金越来越少，但它并不仅仅是为经验丰富的管理者保留的。 新兴风险投资公司占筹集资金的 52.8%，比经验丰富的公司领先去年的总额更高。

本文最初发表于 PitchBook 新闻
